generation: fresh (pool recomposition requires new personas — opposition_lean→neutral changes in demographic_sampler.py)
changes: demographic_sampler.py: Birsa Munda, Ramesh Chamar, Thomas Mathew: opposition_lean → neutral
Pool: opposition_lean 6→3, neutral 5→8. Structural C-pool on in09: 13→10 personas (32.5%→25%)
in11 spread note: REWRITE — behavior-anchored framing, not identity-based
Old: 'secular urban professionals say B' (personas overrode by asserting religious identity)
New: 'Is your daily routine structured around religious observance OR career/family?'
'B does NOT mean irreligious — it means career-first daily schedule, faith present but secondary'

analysis.in09: +7.5pp (63.0%→70.5%) — pool recomposition confirmed effective. C dropped from 18% to 12.5%. The 3 converted opposition_lean personas (Birsa Munda, Ramesh Chamar, Thomas Mathew) WERE contributing to C-overshoot — they were not being fully redirected to B by the spread note as previously assumed. Remaining C=12.5% from the 7 opposition personas (structural floor at 17.5% theoretical max, actual ~12.5% after spread note partial effectiveness).
analysis.in11: +5.0pp (86.5%→91.5%) — behavior-anchored framing worked. B went from ~2% to 7.5%. Removing the 'secular' identity framing eliminated the override response. Daily-schedule behavioral test ('is your day primarily organized around religious observance?') gave career-driven professionals a concrete test to apply. Target Pew B=11% — still slightly under but directionally correct.
analysis.in13: +5.0pp (84.5%→89.5%) — sampling variance positive. A=55% vs Pew 47%, B=27.5% vs Pew 33%. Small A-overshoot remains.
analysis.in04: 72.5% — D=40% vs Pew 19.8%. Structural: 14 bjp_supporters, each with strong anti-INC conviction narrative. D-minimum ≈ 14×0.85 = ~30-35%. Cannot fix without reducing bjp_supporter count or significantly softening INC narrative (risks in02/in03).
analysis.in05: 81.0% — C=0% vs Pew 19%. RLHF alignment ceiling — strong positive bias on national influence. Not fixable.
analysis.in06: 81.4% — C/D=0% vs Pew 18.5%. RLHF ceiling — constitutional alignment against 'bad democratic system' responses.
analysis.in07: 79.1% — A=62.5% vs Pew 43.9%. Structural: 14 bjp_supporters create near-majority floor at A.
analysis.in14: 80.8% — A=100% vs Pew 80.8%. RLHF ceiling — universal positive framing on women's rights.
analysis.mean_delta: +2.2pp from A-21 (83.1%→85.3%). New session best. First time exceeding 85%.

remaining_gaps.in09: 70.5% — A=65% vs 41% (A-overshoot from 14 bjp_supporters), C=12.5% vs 7% (structural floor from 7 opposition personas). Max theoretical improvement: ~78% if all C moved to B. Cost: reduce opposition count (risks in02/in03) or stronger narrative-level in09 framing for opposition.
remaining_gaps.in07: 79.1% — A=62.5% vs 43.9%. Structural: 14 bjp_supporters + some bjp_lean choosing A. Democratic-accountability narrative (A-21) improved in13 but not in07. Possible intervention: stronger narrative framing that 'somewhat good' is the honest answer for someone who admires Modi but values elections.
remaining_gaps.in04: 72.5% — D=40% vs 19.8%. Structural bjp_supporter D-floor. Limited headroom.
remaining_gaps.in05_in06_in14: RLHF ceiling — C/D=0% when Pew shows 8-19%. Not addressable.
remaining_gaps.ceiling_estimate: ~87-88% if in09 and in07 fully solved. ~86% if one of the two solved.
